snippets.title: Division types and precedence
snippets.code:
print(7 / 2)        # 3.5  -> true division is always float
print(7 // 2)       # 3    -> floor division
print(-7 // 2)      # -4   -> rounds toward negative infinity
print(7 % 2)        # 1    -> remainder

print(3 + 2 * 2)    # 7    -> * before +
print(2 ** 3 ** 2)  # 512  -> ** is right-associative: 2 ** (3 ** 2)
snippets.explanation: Know which operator binds first; reach for parentheses when an expression is non-obvious.

snippets.title: Short-circuit and truthiness
snippets.code:
requested = ""
model = requested or "gpt-4o"
print(model)              # 'gpt-4o' -> '' is falsy

items = []
if items:                 # falsy when empty
    print("has items")
else:
    print("empty")        # this runs

print(True and "kept")    # 'kept' -> and returns the second operand here
snippets.explanation: and/or return an operand, not always a pure bool. Empty containers, 0 and '' are falsy.

snippets.title: The 'or default' pitfall with zero
snippets.code:
config = {"temperature": 0.0}

# BUG: 0.0 is falsy, so this becomes 0.7
bad = config.get("temperature") or 0.7
print(bad)   # 0.7  (wrong!)

# Correct: only fall back when the value is truly missing
value = config.get("temperature")
good = value if value is not None else 0.7
print(good)  # 0.0  (correct)
snippets.explanation: Use an explicit 'is not None' check for defaults whenever 0, 0.0 or '' are valid inputs.

snippets.title: Walrus + f-string in a cost loop
snippets.code:
price_per_token = 0.000002
total = 0
for n_tokens in (120, 0, 340):
    if (cost := n_tokens * price_per_token) > 0:   # assign and test in one go
        total += cost
        print(f"+${cost:.6f}")

print(f"Total: ${total:.6f}")
snippets.explanation: The walrus operator (:=) assigns within an expression; f-strings embed and format expressions inline.
